desc: Hadibu is the rough practical base for Socotra rather than the reason to go in itself: supplies, guides, vehicles and island logistics tend to funnel through town before people head out toward dragon blood tree plateaus, lagoons and one of the most distinctive biodiversity landscapes on earth. The natural value is extraordinary, but this is not normal leisure travel and current security advice matters more than climate perfection.

todo.name: Dixam Plateau
todo.desc: Use a local guide to see dragon blood trees and highland views without damaging fragile habitats.

todo.name: Detwah Lagoon
todo.desc: Spend time at the lagoon for turquoise shallows, cliffs and birdlife near Qalansiyah.

todo.name: Hoq Cave
todo.desc: Plan the cave hike with guides, water and heat awareness.

todo.name: Hadibu market
todo.desc: Use the town carefully as the practical base for supplies and island logistics.

wifi.note: Connectivity is limited and inconsistent even by remote-island standards; expect weak hotel setups, patchy mobile data and long stretches where expedition logistics matter far more than being reachable.

prac.fltNote: Hadibu is not a normal book-a-flight destination. Socotra access depends on limited air links, permit and operator arrangements, and the wider Yemen security picture; current UK advice is against all travel to Yemen, so route existence does not make the trip straightforward or currently appropriate for ordinary holiday planning.
prac.visa: British travellers considering Socotra need to understand that FCDO currently advises against all travel to Yemen. Entry rules, permits and practical access should only be assessed in that context, and normal leisure-planning assumptions do not apply.
alerts: FCDO currently advises against all travel to Yemen, including the wider Yemen context around Socotra.
Use local guides and low-impact practices around fragile endemic ecosystems.
Medical support, evacuation options and connectivity are all very limited.

sweet: From a climate-only point of view, the cooler months are the easiest, but that should not be read as a travel recommendation while UK advice remains against all travel to Yemen.
